/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:228:## The save modifier
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:229:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:230:Save wraps any of the four actions' output in a Markdown file. Combine freely with any action.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:231:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:232:Default save paths:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:233:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:234:- Same directory as the input file, with the input's stem plus `.human-eyes-audit.md`, `.human-eyes-suggestions.md`, `.human-eyes-rewrite.md`, or `.human-eyes-write.md`.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:235:- Fallback: `./human-eyes-<action>.md` when there is no input path (e.g., pasted text).
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:236:- Append `-2`, `-3` rather than overwriting an existing file.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:237:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:238:When Save is used:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:239:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:240:- The chat output is a one-line summary of the action's verdict, followed by `Saved to: <path>`, followed by the next-step question if one applies.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:241:- The saved file contains the action's full output template, preceded by a short header:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:242:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:243:```
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:244:# Human-eyes <action> report
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:245:- Input: <input path, or "pasted text", or "brief">
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:246:- Depth: <Balanced / All, or "n/a" for Audit and Suggestions>
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:247:- Date: <ISO 8601>
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:248:```
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:249:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:250:Do not duplicate the full report inline in chat when a saved file exists.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:251:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:252:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:253:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:254:## References
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:255:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:256:- `references/patterns.md`: full catalogue of detected patterns with words to watch and before/after examples. Includes the four most distinctive AI tells (em dashes, manufactured insight, contrived reframes, generic staccato) with their deeper rules and word lists.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:257:- `references/voice.md`: voice-craft for Rewrite and Write. The subtraction problem (Abdulhai et al., 2026): LLM rewrites flatten stance and deplete pronouns; neutrality creeps in even on grammar-only passes. Read during the rewrite or draft step.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:258:- `references/alternatives.md`: vetted human alternatives for lexical patterns, used by Suggestions and by Rewrite or Write when picking replacements.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/SKILL.md:259:- `references/process.md`: required operating procedure for Rewrite and Write. Steps A through E cover structural changes, surface cleanup, structural self-check, semantic preservation, and re-grade revision loops.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:1:# Severity detail: deep-dive on the four most distinctive AI tells
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:2:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:3:Expanded detail on four patterns from the Severity classes table in SKILL.md (em dashes, manufactured insight, adversarial reframes, generic staccato fragments). They get their own subsections because they are the most common and most distinctive AI tells in 2026 — the rules below are the canonical source of truth when these patterns appear.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:4:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:5:Non-negotiable at All depth. At Balanced depth, hard failures and strong warnings must be fixed. Context warnings may be preserved only with explicit disclosure and a concrete reason. Check first, check last, check again.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:6:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:7:## No em dashes
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:8:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:9:At Balanced and All depth, the em dash character must never appear in output. Not once. Not for emphasis, not for asides, not for attribution, not for any reason.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:10:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:11:Replace with commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses. Restructure the sentence if none of those fit. There is always an alternative.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:12:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:13:The only exception is sourced or quoted text where the em dash is part of an author's established style, dialogue rhythm, or period/literary voice. Disclose the preservation explicitly so the user can decide. If the user asked for publication-ready plain prose, normalise them.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:14:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:15:## No manufactured insight
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:16:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:17:Eliminate all framing that performs the act of having a deeper take. AI cannot have insider knowledge, so it compensates with linguistic markers that signal depth without providing any. Either state the observation plainly or remove the sentence if stripping the framing reveals no substance.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:18:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:19:- **False revelation:** "But the real answer is...", "What's actually happening here is...", "Here's what's really going on..."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:20:- **Contrived contrarianism:** "What nobody is talking about...", "What no one is talking about...", "Contrary to popular belief...", "The uncomfortable truth is..."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:21:- **Unnoticed-shift framing:** "When no one noticed...", "The shift nobody noticed...", "Before anyone noticed...", "Without anyone noticing..." when used to manufacture secret significance.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:22:- **Pseudo-profundity:** "quietly revolutionary", "quietly became", "the quiet part", "subtly", "understated", "unassuming" when used to inflate significance
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:23:- **Performed knowingness:** "If you know, you know", "And that changes everything", "Let that sink in", "Read that again", "And honestly?", "Here's the thing:"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:24:- **Formulaic depth framing:** "The reason is straightforward:", "The reason is simple:", "Here's why:", "What's strange is", "What's interesting is", "What's remarkable is". These perform the act of having an explanation before delivering one.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:25:- **Dramatic narrative transitions:** "Something shifted.", "Everything changed.", "And then, everything clicked.", "That's when it hit me." These claim a turning point without earning it.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:26:- **Contrived contrast:** "This isn't X. It's Y." as a standalone dramatic beat (e.g. "This isn't a bug. It's a feature." or "This isn't a character flaw. It's hardware."). The two-sentence snap format is a reliable AI fingerprint regardless of content.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:27:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:28:These patterns are non-negotiable when they are generic assistant performance. A funny piece with "And honestly?" may still be carrying an AI tell, but a voiced essay may use similar phrasing intentionally. Apply the purpose test at Balanced depth; remove at All depth unless the phrase clearly belongs to the writer's voice.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:29:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:30:## No adversarial reframes
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:31:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:32:Do not satisfy the letter of a rule while keeping the same AI-shaped move. This especially applies to pattern 9, contrived contrast / negative parallelism. If the draft says "It's not X, it's Y", an acceptable rewrite is not "It is Y, not X" or "Beyond X, it becomes Y". Those are the same device with the furniture moved around.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:33:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:34:Flag and rewrite all of these as direct claims:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:35:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:36:- "It's not X, it's Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:37:- "It's Y, not X."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:38:- "Less X than Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:39:- "More Y than X."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:40:- "Not so much X as Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:41:- "Beyond X, it is Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:42:- "You might think X. Actually, Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:43:- "No X. No Y. Just Z."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:44:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:45:The test is semantic: if the sentence creates drama by rejecting a plain interpretation and revealing a supposedly deeper abstraction, remove the setup and state the actual claim with concrete support.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:46:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:47:## No staccato fragments
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:48:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:49:Short sentences used in sequence for dramatic effect are often an AI tell. "That matters." or "Full stop." or "This is it." as standalone beats in generic articles are usually generated.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:50:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:51:A sentence should be as long as the thought it contains. When you find fragments used for generic emphasis, fold their content into a neighbouring sentence or expand into something that earns its own line. Preserve staccato when it is character voice, comedy, panic, dialogue, or deliberate literary rhythm.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:52:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:53:### Before
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:54:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:55:> The results were clear. Strikingly so. The model outperformed every baseline. Every single one. And nobody saw it coming.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:56:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:57:### After
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:58:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/severity-detail.md:59:> The results were unambiguous: the model outperformed every baseline by a significant margin, which surprised most of the team.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:1:1
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:2:I walked into the session expecting a tools demo. What I got instead was a set of organising instincts—patterns the Loomio founders have learned the hard way while building software for groups that don’t rely on hierarchy to get things done. The throughline was simple: decentralisation doesn’t happen by default. It’s designed, practised, and maintained.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:3:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:4:Below are the eight patterns as they were taught, with what landed for me in the room and how we’ve started to apply them.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:5:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:6:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:7:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:8:1. Intentionally produce counter-culture (“belonging is a superpower”)
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:9:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:10:The facilitators were explicit: if you want a decentralised group, you have to create a culture that makes it possible. That means designing for belonging—rituals, language, onboarding, and signals that say “you’re allowed to act here”.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:11:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:12:In the workshop, this showed up in small things: rounds where everyone spoke early, norms that prioritised listening over performance, and an assumption that newcomers could contribute meaningfully on day one. It felt engineered, not accidental.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:13:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:14:What we took away is that “culture” isn’t a backdrop. It’s an active system. If you don’t build it, the default culture (often hierarchical, often exclusionary) fills the gap.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:15:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:16:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:17:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:18:2. Systematically distribute care labour (stewards and stewardees)
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:19:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:20:Care work—checking in, following up, smoothing conflict—keeps groups alive, but it’s usually invisible and unevenly carried. The Loomio model pairs stewards with stewardees, making care a role with structure, not an afterthought.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:21:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:22:In the session, we mapped who currently holds care in our own group. The pattern was predictable: a small number of people doing most of it. Naming that out loud shifted the conversation from “people should step up” to “the system needs to change”.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:23:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:24:The steward/stewardee framing gives you a lightweight mechanism to distribute that load. It also legitimises care as work, which matters if you want it to be sustained.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:25:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:26:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:27:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:28:3. Make explicit norms and boundaries
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:29:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:30:“Unwritten rules are just rules some people don’t know yet.” That line stuck.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:31:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:32:The facilitators pushed us to externalise norms—how decisions are made, how conflict is handled, what’s in scope and what isn’t. Not as a static document, but as a living set of agreements that can be revised.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:33:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:34:We did a quick exercise where each group wrote down three norms we assume everyone knows. Then we swapped lists. The gaps were obvious. What felt “obvious” internally wasn’t obvious at all.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:35:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:36:The practical shift is to treat norms like infrastructure: visible, versioned, and easy to reference.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:37:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:38:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:39:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:40:4. Keep talking about power
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:41:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:42:Decentralisation doesn’t eliminate power; it redistributes it. If you stop naming power, it recentralises quietly.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:43:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:44:This pattern was less comfortable, which is probably the point. We were asked to identify where power actually sits in our groups—who sets agendas, who has context, who controls access to tools or information. The answers weren’t always aligned with formal roles.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:45:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:46:The facilitators modelled a simple discipline: revisit power regularly, not just when something breaks. That normalises the conversation and reduces the friction of raising it.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:47:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:48:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:49:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:50:5. Make decisions asynchronously
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:51:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:52:If decisions only happen in meetings, you privilege the people who can attend and perform in that format. Asynchronous decision-making widens participation and gives people time to think.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:53:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:54:The Loomio approach structures this: proposals, time-bound input, clear decision rules. In the workshop, we ran a short async-style decision in the room—draft a proposal, gather reactions, iterate, then decide. Even compressed, it produced a more considered outcome than a typical “any objections?” moment.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:55:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:56:The key is discipline. Async only works if people know when to engage, how to respond, and when a decision closes.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:57:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:58:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:59:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:60:6. Agree how you use tech (the “holy trinity”: realtime / async / static)
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:61:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:62:Most groups accumulate tools without agreement. The result is fragmentation: decisions in chat, context in documents, history lost in threads.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:63:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:64:The “holy trinity” frames three modes:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:65:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:66:	•	Realtime (e.g. chat, meetings) for rapid exchange
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:67:	•	Async (e.g. forums, decision tools) for considered input and decisions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:68:	•	Static (e.g. docs, wikis) for durable knowledge
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:69:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:70:The pattern is to agree what each mode is for—and stick to it. In the workshop, we mapped our current tools against these modes. The overlaps and gaps were obvious.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:71:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:72:Our immediate change was simple: stop making decisions in chat. Use async spaces for decisions, and static spaces for the final record.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:73:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:74:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:75:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:76:7. Use rhythm to cut information overload
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:77:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:78:Information overload isn’t just volume; it’s unpredictability. Rhythm—regular cycles of activity—helps people know when to pay attention.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:79:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:80:The facilitators shared examples: weekly check-ins, monthly retros, quarterly planning. Not as meetings for their own sake, but as anchors that structure the flow of work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:81:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:82:We tested this by sketching a basic cadence for our group. Even a rough rhythm reduced the sense of constant interruption. It created “edges” around work, which is what people need to manage attention.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:83:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:84:The insight here is that rhythm is a coordination tool. It reduces cognitive load without centralising control.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:85:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:86:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:87:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:88:8. Generate new patterns together
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:89:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:90:The final pattern is meta: don’t just adopt patterns—create them. Decentralised groups need the capability to evolve their own practices.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:91:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:92:The workshop itself modelled this. After introducing the patterns, the facilitators had us adapt them to our contexts, then share back what we’d changed. Some groups merged patterns; others added constraints or rituals.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:93:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:94:What mattered wasn’t fidelity to the original model, but the act of collective design. That’s what builds ownership and keeps the system adaptable.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:95:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:96:⸻
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:97:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:98:What changed for us
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:99:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:100:The workshop didn’t give us a finished operating model. It gave us a set of levers.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:101:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:102:We left with a few concrete shifts:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:103:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:104:	•	Care is now explicit. We’ve started pairing stewards and stewardees, with a simple expectation of regular check-ins.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:105:	•	Decisions have a home. We’re moving proposals into an async space with clear timelines, and documenting outcomes in a static location.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:106:	•	Norms are visible. We’ve drafted a short set of working agreements and put them somewhere everyone can find (and edit).
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:107:	•	We’re naming power earlier. Not perfectly, but more often, and with less hesitation.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:108:	•	We’re testing a rhythm. A lightweight weekly and monthly cadence to stabilise attention.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:109:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:110:None of this is dramatic. That’s the point. Decentralisation is built from small, repeatable practices.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:111:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:112:Friction and limits
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:113:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:114:A few constraints are worth naming.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:115:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:116:	•	Discipline is required. Async decision-making and tool agreements only work if people follow them. Without that, things drift back to the easiest path (usually chat).
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:117:	•	Time to adopt. Making norms explicit and redistributing care takes effort upfront. It can feel slower before it feels better.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:118:	•	Power doesn’t disappear. Even with good patterns, informal power persists. The work of noticing and rebalancing is ongoing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:119:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:120:The facilitators didn’t present these as problems to solve once. They’re conditions to manage continuously.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:121:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:122:Closing reflection
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:123:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:124:What stayed with me most is the framing of decentralisation as a design problem, not a personality trait. You don’t get it by hiring the right people or declaring flatness. You get it by building systems—cultural, social, and technical—that make distributed action viable.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:125:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:126:“Belonging is a superpower” sounded soft at first. By the end, it felt operational. If people feel they belong, they act. If they act, work distributes. If work distributes, hierarchy loosens.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:127:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:128:That’s the loop these patterns are trying to sustain.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:129:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:130:2
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:131:Workshops as portals
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:132:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:133:And how to create clarity in consulting work
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:134:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:135:Halfway through the morning, someone in the room says the thing everyone has been avoiding.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:136:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:137:“So are we actually trying to fix the onboarding process, or are we trying to decide who owns it?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:138:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:139:There is usually a small silence after a question like that. Not an awkward silence, exactly. More like the sound of a group recalibrating. People look at each other. Someone leans back. Someone else gives a tiny nod, because yes, that is the real question. The slide on the wall says “current-state journey map”, but the work has moved somewhere more useful. The room has crossed the threshold from performance into truth.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:140:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:141:This is why I like workshops.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:142:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:143:Not because they are fun. Not because they produce sticky notes. Not because everyone gets to feel included, though that can matter. I like workshops because they are portals. They move a client and a consultant from abstract possibility into a shared experience of working together. They show the client how you think. They show you how the client makes decisions. They reveal whether the problem is clear, whether the group has authority, whether the politics are manageable, and whether there is enough trust to do the larger piece of work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:144:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:145:A good workshop is not a miniature version of the solution. It is a charged, time-boxed way to create clarity. That clarity is valuable in its own right. It is not a free prelude to the real work. It is consulting work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:146:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:147:This is one of the most useful lessons I have learned as a consultant: you do not need to sell the whole transformation, research program, operating model, content strategy or service redesign upfront. Often, you should not. You can sell the first intelligent container. A one-day workshop is often the best container there is.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:148:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:149:The inside/outside problem
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:150:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:151:Consulting has a persistent inside/outside problem.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:152:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:153:The client knows the organisation from the inside. They understand the history, the personalities, the prior attempts, the acronyms, the informal rules, the sensitivities, the teams that do not talk to each other, and the executive who has already formed a view. They know more than you do.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:154:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:155:The consultant comes from the outside. You bring pattern recognition, method, pace, independence, external examples, and the ability to ask questions that insiders may no longer be allowed to ask. You can see things that have become normalised inside the system. But you do not yet understand the local terrain.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:156:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:157:Both sides are slightly exposed at the beginning.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:158:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:159:The client is wondering: will this person get it? Will they understand our context without making us explain everything from first principles? Will they be useful, or will they sell us a generic process? Will they make us look foolish in front of our stakeholders?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:160:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:161:The consultant is wondering: is this the stated problem or the real problem? Does the sponsor have authority? Are the constraints real or inherited? Is there a decision to be made, or just an appetite for activity? Is this work feasible?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:162:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:163:A proposal can only answer so much. A credentials deck answers even less. A workshop answers something more important: how does it feel to work together?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:164:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:165:That question is not soft. It is commercial. It tells both parties whether there is enough shared language, confidence and momentum to proceed. It also prevents one of the most common failures in consulting: selling a large engagement before anyone has understood the shape of the problem.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:166:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:167:A workshop lets the relationship become real without pretending everything is already known.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:168:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:169:Clarity is a deliverable
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:170:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:171:Consultants often undercharge for clarity because they confuse it with preparation.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:172:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:173:They treat discovery as something that happens before the paid work. They have a few unpaid conversations, diagnose too much too early, write a proposal full of assumptions, and hope the client agrees. Then the project begins, and everyone discovers that the assumptions were incomplete.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:174:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:175:A better model is to make clarity the first paid outcome.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:176:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:177:The point of a one-day workshop is not to solve the problem. It is to create a shared understanding of the problem, the decision context, the constraints, the stakeholders, and the next sensible move. That is not a lesser outcome. In many organisations, that is the work nobody has been able to do.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:178:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:179:Clarity matters for the client because it reduces internal fog. A team may have been carrying a problem for months, but carrying it in fragments. One person sees a governance issue. Another sees a technology issue. Another sees a resourcing issue. Another sees a leadership issue. Another sees a “comms problem”, which often means nobody has agreed what is true.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:180:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:181:The workshop makes the implicit visible. It gives the group a way to see the problem as a system rather than as competing grievances. It also creates a record: what we know, what we do not know, what we disagree on, what matters most, and what we are going to do next.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:182:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:183:Clarity matters for the consultant because it protects the work. It stops you from accepting a vague brief and turning it into an overconfident scope. It lets you test the client’s appetite for evidence, decisions and trade-offs. It shows you where the risks are before you are committed to delivery.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:184:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:185:A workshop also gives you something no written brief can provide: behavioural data.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:186:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:187:Who speaks first? Who keeps returning to risk? Who reframes everything as a technology issue? Who has authority but stays silent? Who is treated as difficult, but is actually naming the constraint? Who asks practical questions? Who performs certainty? Who is absent, and what does their absence tell you?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:188:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:189:This information changes the way you scope the work. It often changes whether you want the work at all.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:190:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:191:Do not sell the workshop as the solution
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:192:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:193:A mistake I see consultants make is trying to sell a one-day workshop as though it will fix something.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:194:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:195:“We’ll solve your strategy.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:196:“We’ll align the team.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:197:“We’ll map the whole experience.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:198:“We’ll define the operating model.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:199:“We’ll create the roadmap.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:200:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:201:No, you probably will not. Not properly. Not in a day.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:202:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:203:A one-day workshop can create the conditions for those things. It can clarify the decision to be made. It can surface assumptions. It can establish priorities. It can identify evidence gaps. It can generate options. It can produce a draft direction. It can build enough shared confidence to move into a larger engagement.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:204:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:205:But if you promise resolution, you will either over-facilitate the room into false agreement or disappoint the client when reality proves more complex.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:206:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:207:The more honest position is stronger: this workshop will create clarity about the problem, the stakeholders, the decision points and the next phase of work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:208:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:209:That is chargeable. That is useful. That is a legitimate consulting outcome.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:210:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:211:The language matters. I try to avoid selling a workshop as “collaborative ideation” or “a fun session to get everyone aligned”. That makes it sound optional. Instead, I describe it as a structured working session to establish the current state, clarify the target outcome, identify constraints and agree the next decision pathway.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:212:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:213:This frames the workshop as serious work. It also attracts better participants. People understand they are not attending a morale exercise. They are there to think, decide and expose what needs to be resolved.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:214:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:215:Structure for outcomes, not happiness
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:216:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:217:A workshop can feel good and still be useless.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:218:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:219:This is a harsh truth, but it saves a lot of trouble. Participants may enjoy the session because it is different from their usual meetings, because they get to speak, because the facilitator is competent, or because the catering is decent. Enjoyment is not the same as progress.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:220:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:221:The question is not “did people like the workshop?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:222:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:223:The question is “what changed because the workshop happened?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:224:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:225:Sometimes what changes is a decision. Sometimes it is a reframed problem. Sometimes it is agreement about scope. Sometimes it is the discovery that the client is not ready to proceed. Sometimes it is the recognition that three teams are using the same word to mean different things.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:226:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:227:Those are outcomes.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:228:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:229:When designing a workshop, I start with the exit conditions. By the end of the day, what must be clearer than it is now? What must the sponsor be able to say? What must the group understand? What must be documented? What decision does this workshop enable, even if it does not make the decision itself?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:230:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:231:That usually leads to a simple structure.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:232:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:233:First, establish the frame. Why are we here? What decision or uncertainty does this session support? What is in scope? What is out of scope? What will not be resolved today?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:234:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:235:Second, build the shared picture. What is happening now? What evidence do we have? What are people experiencing? Where are the pain points, risks or missed opportunities? What do we know versus assume?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:236:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:237:Third, expose the tensions. Where do participants disagree? What constraints matter? What trade-offs are unavoidable? What has been tried before? What would make this fail?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:238:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:239:Fourth, create direction. What options are available? What criteria should be used to assess them? What is the most sensible next step? What needs further investigation?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:240:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:241:Finally, close with commitments. Who needs to be involved next? What decisions are required? What artefact will come out of the session? What will the consultant do, and what does the client need to provide?
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:242:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:243:This is not complicated, but it is disciplined. Without that discipline, workshops become a sequence of activities rather than a progression of thinking.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:244:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:245:The workshop is a diagnostic instrument
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:246:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:247:The workshop itself tells you what kind of consulting work may be needed next.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:248:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:249:For example, if the group cannot agree on the problem, the next phase may need to be discovery, research or diagnostic work. If the problem is clear but ownership is confused, the next phase may need to focus on governance. If there is strong agreement but no evidence, the next phase may need to test assumptions with users, staff or data. If everyone knows the answer but nobody has permission to act, the issue is probably sponsorship or decision rights.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:250:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:251:This is why workshops are such effective entry points. They do not just produce outputs. They reveal the nature of the work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:252:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:253:In one project, I was asked to run a session that was initially described as a content planning workshop. The client wanted to make a large website easier to use, and the assumed activity was to map content types and page priorities. Once we got into the room, it became obvious that the content problem was real but downstream. The deeper issue was ownership. Different business areas were publishing content to meet local needs, but nobody had authority over the whole user experience. Search was poor, navigation was inconsistent, and users were encountering the organisation as a set of internal divisions rather than as a coherent service.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:254:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:255:The workshop did not fix the website. It did something more useful at that stage: it clarified that a content uplift alone would fail without a governance model. That changed the next piece of work. The client did not need a better page template first. They needed decision rights, publishing standards, and a clearer model for who owned the digital front door.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:256:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:257:In another piece of work, a client came in wanting help designing a program for a new audience. The first conversation was full of good intent but too many assumptions. A one-day workshop helped us separate the policy ambition from the delivery mechanism. We mapped the audience’s likely barriers, the client’s constraints, the evidence already available, and the parts that still needed co-design. By the end, the most valuable output was not a polished program concept. It was a set of questions the organisation had to answer before investing in delivery.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:258:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:259:That sounds modest. It was not. It prevented premature design. It saved the client from building a program around internal enthusiasm rather than participant reality.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:260:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:261:Useful prompts for jam-sessions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:262:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:263:Some workshops need formal activities. Others need well-held conversation.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:264:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:265:I use “jam-session” to describe a working conversation with enough structure to stay productive but enough openness to let the real material emerge. These sessions are useful early in consulting work, especially when the client knows a lot but has not yet organised that knowledge into a clear brief.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:266:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:267:The facilitator’s job is to keep the conversation moving between concrete evidence and useful abstraction. Too concrete, and the room drowns in detail. Too abstract, and everyone agrees with words that do not mean anything.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:268:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:269:A few prompts I return to:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:270:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:271:“What is the problem this work is meant to change?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:272:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:273:Not “what is the project?” Not “what are we building?” Start with the change. If the group cannot answer this, the brief is not ready.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:274:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:275:“What makes this hard here?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:276:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:277:This prevents generic diagnosis. Most problems have a local shape. The method may be familiar, but the constraints are specific.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:278:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:279:“What has already been tried?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:280:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:281:This respects the client’s history and avoids accidentally recommending something that failed two years ago. It also surfaces organisational scar tissue.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:282:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:283:“What do people disagree about?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:284:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:285:This is often more useful than asking what people agree on. Agreement can be polite. Disagreement is informative.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:286:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:287:“What would make this work fail?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:288:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:289:This prompt changes the energy in the room. People are often more honest when asked about failure than when asked about success.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:290:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:291:“Who needs this to be true?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:292:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:293:This is useful when a claim keeps appearing without evidence. It helps separate fact from institutional preference.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:294:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:295:“What decision does this enable?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:296:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:297:This keeps the workshop from becoming an exercise in discussion. Consulting work should eventually support a decision, even if that decision is to investigate further.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:298:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:299:“What would we need to know to proceed with confidence?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:300:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:301:This prompt is especially useful when the room wants certainty too early. It turns anxiety into an evidence plan.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:302:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:303:“What is out of scope, and are we comfortable with that?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:304:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:305:Scope is not just what you include. It is what you exclude without later pretending you forgot.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:306:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:307:“What is the smallest useful next step?”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:308:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:309:This helps when the group is overwhelmed. Not every next step needs to be dramatic. It needs to be real.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:310:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:311:These prompts work because they are not clever. They are plain. They create pressure in the right places. They also make the consultant’s value visible. You are not there to dominate the room with frameworks. You are there to help the room think better than it could without you.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:312:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:313:The artefact matters
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:314:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:315:A workshop should produce something after the room clears.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:316:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:317:Not a transcript. Not photographs of sticky notes dumped into a slide deck. Not a decorative summary that says everyone had a productive discussion.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:318:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:319:The artefact should capture the clarity created.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:320:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:321:Depending on the work, this might be a concise findings note, a problem statement, a decision brief, a current-state map, a prioritised opportunity list, a draft roadmap, a scope recommendation, or a proposed next-phase plan.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:322:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:323:The artefact is where the consulting value becomes portable. It allows the sponsor to brief upward, socialise the work, secure funding, or decide not to proceed. It also protects against the workshop becoming a pleasant memory rather than a business input.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:324:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:325:I usually think about the artefact before designing the session. If the final output is a decision brief, the workshop needs to generate decision criteria. If the output is a discovery plan, the workshop needs to expose assumptions and evidence gaps. If the output is a roadmap, the workshop needs to clarify dependencies and sequencing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:326:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:327:This also helps with pricing. The client is not paying for seven hours in a room. They are paying for preparation, facilitation, synthesis, judgement and a usable artefact.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:328:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:329:Do not undersell the day
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:330:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:331:A one-day workshop should not be priced like a casual meeting.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:332:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:333:If you are a consultant with meaningful expertise, $4,000 for a day is not outrageous. In many contexts, it is low. The day itself may be seven hours, but the work is not.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:334:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:335:You need to understand the context, design the session, prepare materials, manage the room, adapt in real time, synthesise the outputs, and produce something the client can use. You are also bringing years of pattern recognition into that room. The client is not buying time. They are buying compression.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:336:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:337:A well-run workshop can save weeks of circular internal discussion. It can prevent a poorly scoped project. It can reveal that the client needs a different intervention. It can create the confidence required to fund the next stage. It can also show that a larger engagement would be wasteful or premature.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:338:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:339:That is valuable.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:340:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:341:Underpricing creates other problems too. It signals that the workshop is lightweight. It attracts clients who treat it as a brainstorming session rather than a serious working session. It also pressures the consultant to over-sell the next phase because the first phase was not commercially viable.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:342:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:343:A properly priced workshop lets you be more honest. You can sell the day as its own useful engagement, not as a loss leader. If more work follows, good. If it does not, the client still receives value and you have not subsidised their indecision.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:344:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:345:The workshop as mutual audition
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:346:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:347:There is another reason workshops work well as an entry point: they are mutual auditions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:348:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:349:The client is testing you. That is fair. They want to know whether you can hold complexity, work with their people, understand their language, and create momentum without bulldozing the room.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:350:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:351:You are also testing the client. That is equally fair. You want to know whether they can make decisions, tolerate ambiguity, provide access to the right people, and engage with evidence. You want to know whether the sponsor will protect the work or disappear when trade-offs appear.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:352:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:353:This does not need to be cynical. It is responsible.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:354:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:355:Some clients are not ready for the work they think they want. Some are ready, but for a different piece of work. Some have a strong sponsor and a messy problem, which is often workable. Some have a neat brief and no authority, which is often not.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:356:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:357:A workshop reveals these things quickly.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:358:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:359:I have left workshops more confident that the client needed a substantial program of work. I have also left workshops thinking the best recommendation was to pause, gather evidence, or narrow the scope. That recommendation can feel commercially counterintuitive, but it builds trust. It also prevents the consultant from inheriting a problem that nobody is willing to own.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:360:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:361:What the client is really buying
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:362:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:363:When a client buys a workshop, they are not only buying facilitation.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:364:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:365:They are buying a temporary thinking environment.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:366:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:367:Inside that environment, the normal patterns are interrupted. The urgent gives way to the important. People who usually communicate through documents or meetings are asked to think together. Assumptions become visible. Decisions become explicit. Ambiguity gets named rather than carried.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:368:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:369:The consultant’s job is to design and hold that environment.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:370:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:371:That means paying attention to pace, evidence, authority, participation and synthesis. It means knowing when to let the room explore and when to intervene. It means not mistaking volume for insight. It means protecting quieter forms of expertise. It means noticing when the group is avoiding the real issue.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:372:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:373:It also means resisting the temptation to make everyone happy.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:374:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:375:Some of the best workshops involve discomfort. Not hostility, not confusion, not theatrical conflict. Just the discomfort of being more precise than usual. The discomfort of admitting that the problem is not the one written in the brief. The discomfort of realising that a favourite solution has no clear owner. The discomfort of seeing that “alignment” has been used to cover unresolved decisions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:376:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:377:That discomfort is often the portal.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:378:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:379:A workshop is not a trick
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:380:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:381:I do not see workshops as a sales trick. They are not a way to smuggle in a larger engagement. They are a way to begin in proportion to what is known.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:382:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:383:Sometimes the next step after a workshop is a major discovery phase. Sometimes it is a small advisory engagement. Sometimes it is a roadmap. Sometimes it is a recommendation that the client do internal work before bringing in external support. The point is not to force a predetermined path. The point is to create enough clarity that the next path is credible.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:384:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:385:This is why the one-day workshop is such a useful consulting offer. It is contained, understandable and low-risk compared with a large engagement. But it is not trivial. It gives the client something they need and gives the consultant enough exposure to scope responsibly.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:386:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:387:It bridges the inside/outside problem by creating a shared inside, even briefly. For one day, the consultant is no longer looking at the organisation through a brief, and the client is no longer assessing the consultant through a proposal. Both are in the work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:388:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:389:That changes the relationship.

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:393:By the end of a good workshop, the room should feel different. Not euphoric. Clearer.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:394:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:395:People should be able to say what the work is really about. The consultant should have enough information to recommend a sensible next step.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:396:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:397:That is the threshold.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:398:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:399:A workshop is a portal because it moves everyone through that threshold together. Before it, the work is still mostly imagined. After it, the work has a shape. It may be larger than expected, smaller than expected, or different from what was first described. But it is no longer vague.

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:408:What MDMA Therapy Did For Me
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:409:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:410:I never thought I’d be lying on a sofa, blindfolded, clutching the hand of a therapist I’d met barely a week before, as warm waves of chemically induced empathy peeled away decades of self-defence. But there I was, tears soaking through my mask, muttering softly, “Holy shit, I’m actually okay.”
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:411:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:412:Let’s rewind.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:413:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:414:Before I stepped into MDMA-assisted therapy, the whole idea felt like something only privileged Californians or fringe psychonauts would entertain. I’d spent years navigating traditional talk therapy—battling childhood trauma, fucked-up attachment patterns, and a persistent sense of inadequacy—but those sessions, despite their usefulness, felt incomplete. They were missing something visceral, something I couldn’t access through mere words.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:415:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:416:The stigma was real. The moment I told a friend I was considering MDMA therapy, their face twisted in concern. “You’re going to take ecstasy with a therapist?” they asked, half-amused, half-horrified. It sounded irresponsible at best, dangerous at worst. Yet beneath my own scepticism, something resonated—a quiet, insistent voice telling me I needed a different approach.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:417:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:418:Eventually, curiosity and desperation outweighed the discomfort of judgment. My anxiety had reached critical levels; my marriage, though loving, was fraying under the weight of my unresolved emotional baggage. I was tired of flinching whenever someone hugged me, of overreacting to innocent comments, of carrying shame like a fucking birthmark. I needed a catalyst for change.

My two therapists—compassionate, steady, and deeply trained in trauma-informed care—sat nearby, offering reassuring smiles as they explained the process.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:423:

The therapeutic space was safe, private, and comforting. Soft blankets, ambient music, and fresh flowers created a cocoon-like atmosphere, contrasting sharply with my sterile preconceptions of medicalised interventions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:425:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:426:After thorough medical screening and prep sessions, the day arrived. I swallowed 120 milligrams of pharmaceutical-grade MDMA with ceremonial gravitas, feeling a mixture of apprehension and cautious hope. They placed headphones over my ears, covered my eyes with a soft mask, and invited me inward.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:427:

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:429:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:430:Into the Heart of Trauma
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:431:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:432:MDMA allowed me to face my childhood without recoiling. Memories surfaced gently, carrying their emotional weight without overwhelming me. Rather than dissociating or numbing, I was fully present. I saw clearly, maybe for the first time, how early neglect and inconsistent caregiving had shaped my fears of abandonment and rejection. I recognised how these buried wounds had distorted my perceptions, influencing my marriage and friendships.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:433:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/generated ai personal essays.txt:434:The therapists guided me softly, asking gentle questions, validating my experiences without imposing narratives. For the first time, compassion—true, visceral compassion—for my younger self emerged effortlessly. The little kid who’d felt forgotten and unworthy was no longer someone I rejected or pitied; she was simply a part of me deserving of love and understanding.

My attachment anxieties loosened their grip, not because the past changed, but because my relationship to it had shifted profoundly.
