/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:18:## Evidence hierarchy from the reference audit
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:19:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:20:Use source strength when deciding severity. The ruleset should surface clusters of suspicious writing behaviours, not claim that one phrase proves AI authorship.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:21:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:22:**Strong empirical backbone:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:23:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:24:- Kobak / Science Advances and the `llm-excess-vocab` dataset support lexical spike detection at corpus level, especially in scientific prose. Use vocabulary as density and clustering evidence, not as one-word proof. Source: https://github.com/berenslab/llm-excess-vocab
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:25:- Juzek and Ward's "Why does ChatGPT delve so much?" supports a narrow scientific-abstract vocabulary signal. It is credible for excess-word lists but not for confident document-level claims. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.11385
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:26:- GPTZero's 2026 paper supports the architecture: hierarchical signals, granular findings, adversarial testing, and transparent mixed evidence. It does not add a prose-style rule by itself. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.13042
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:27:- Stanford HAI's detector-bias reporting supports warnings over accusations, especially for non-native English writers. Use process evidence and user review rather than detector-style verdicts. Source: https://hai.stanford.edu/news/ai-detectors-biased-against-non-native-english-writers
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:28:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:29:**Useful but tentative style sources:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:30:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:31:- GPTZero's AI Vocabulary page is useful as a phrase list, but its own framing separates vocabulary scanning from AI-probability scoring. Use all 100 public table rows only as clustering signals. Source: https://gptzero.me/ai-vocabulary
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:32:- Shreya Shankar's AI Writing essay is one of the strongest craft references: bad sentence subjects, orphaned demonstratives, empty paragraph endings, over-bulleting, flat rhythm, low information density, vagueness, and fluency without understanding. These directly support the self-audit and several programmatic checks. Source: https://www.sh-reya.com/blog/ai-writing/
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:33:- OpenAI's April 2025 GPT-4o sycophancy rollback supports hard-failing assistant flattery and fake affirmation such as "great question" and "you're absolutely right." Source: https://openai.com/index/sycophancy-in-gpt-4o/
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:34:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:35:**Domain and provenance signals:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:36:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:37:- Walsh, Preus, and Gronski support poetry-specific manual checks: constrained uniformity, rhyme/quatrain defaults, first-person plural overuse, and mood-word clusters. Keep this genre-specific. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15299
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:38:- Clarkesworld reporting supports fiction as a submission/provenance problem and a manual craft-audit area. Use it for flat dialogue, exposition, pacing, lack of subtext, and over-resolved endings, not as a global regex rule. Source: https://www.npr.org/2023/02/24/1159286436/ai-chatbot-chatgpt-magazine-clarkesworld-artificial-intelligence
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:39:- Futurism's Sports Illustrated reporting supports journalism and review-provenance checks: fake bylines, fake bios, AI headshots, affiliate product-review sludge, undisclosed generated content, and byline laundering. Source: https://futurism.com/sports-illustrated-ai-generated-writers
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:40:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:41:**2026 operating stance:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:42:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:43:- Em dashes are still used by human writers, but in publication-ready plain prose they are now a strong AI-style signal. Treat them as strong warnings. They must be removed at every depth (Balanced and All); preserve only when the source genuinely uses them stylistically and the preservation is disclosed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:44:- The best signals are clusters: GPTZero/Kobak vocabulary density, contrived contrast laundering, empty endings, vague demonstrative starts, placeholder residue, sycophantic assistant residue, unrequested headings/lists/Unicode flair, paragraph uniformity, generic email closers, and fake citations or provenance artifacts.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:45:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:46:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:47:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:48:## Content patterns
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:49:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:50:### 1. Significance inflation
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:51:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:52:**Words to watch:** stands/serves as, is a testament/reminder, a vital/significant/crucial/pivotal/key role/moment, underscores/highlights its importance, reflects broader, symbolizing its ongoing/enduring/lasting, setting the stage for, marking/shaping the, represents/marks a shift, key turning point, evolving landscape, indelible mark, deeply rooted, remarkably, strikingly, staggering/staggeringly
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:53:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:54:Inflates importance by claiming things "represent" or "contribute to" broader trends without explaining why anyone should care.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:55:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:56:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:57:> The Statistical Institute of Catalonia was officially established in 1989, marking a pivotal moment in the evolution of regional statistics in Spain. This initiative was part of a broader movement across Spain to decentralize administrative functions and enhance regional governance.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:58:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:59:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:60:> The Statistical Institute of Catalonia was established in 1989 to collect and publish regional statistics independently from Spain's national statistics office.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:61:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:62:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:63:### 2. Notability claims
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:64:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:65:**Words to watch:** independent coverage, local/regional/national media outlets, written by a leading expert, active social media presence
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:66:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:67:Asserts notability by listing sources without context, as though the mention itself is the story.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:68:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:69:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:70:> Her views have been cited in The New York Times, BBC, Financial Times, and The Hindu. She maintains an active social media presence with over 500,000 followers.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:71:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:72:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:73:> In a 2024 New York Times interview, she argued that AI regulation should focus on outcomes rather than methods.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:74:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:75:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:76:### 3. Superficial -ing analyses
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:77:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:78:**Words to watch:** highlighting/underscoring/emphasizing..., ensuring..., reflecting/symbolizing..., contributing to..., cultivating/fostering..., encompassing..., showcasing...
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:79:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:80:Present participle phrases tacked onto sentences to simulate analytical depth without adding information.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:81:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:82:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:83:> The temple's color palette of blue, green, and gold resonates with the region's natural beauty, symbolizing Texas bluebonnets, the Gulf of Mexico, and the diverse Texan landscapes, reflecting the community's deep connection to the land.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:84:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:85:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:86:> The temple uses blue, green, and gold. According to the architect, the colours were chosen to reference local bluebonnets and the Gulf coast.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:87:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:88:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:89:### 4. Promotional language
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:90:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:91:**Words to watch:** boasts a, vibrant, rich (figurative), profound, enhancing its, showcasing, exemplifies, commitment to, natural beauty, nestled, in the heart of, groundbreaking (figurative), renowned, breathtaking, must-visit, stunning
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:92:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:93:Reads like tourism marketing rather than description.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:94:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:95:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:96:> Nestled within the breathtaking region of Gonder in Ethiopia, Alamata Raya Kobo stands as a vibrant town with a rich cultural heritage and stunning natural beauty.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:97:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:98:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:99:> Alamata Raya Kobo is a town in the Gonder region of Ethiopia, known for its weekly market and 18th-century church.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:100:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:101:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:102:### 5. Vague attributions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:103:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:104:**Words to watch:** Industry reports, Observers have cited, Experts argue, Some critics argue, several sources/publications (when few cited)
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:105:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:106:Attributes opinions to vague authorities without specific sources, creating illusions of consensus.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:107:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:108:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:109:> Due to its unique characteristics, the Haolai River is of interest to researchers and conservationists. Experts believe it plays a crucial role in the regional ecosystem.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:110:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:111:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:112:> The Haolai River supports several endemic fish species, according to a 2019 survey by the Chinese Academy of Sciences.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:113:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:114:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:115:### 6. Formulaic challenges sections
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:116:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:117:**Words to watch:** Despite its... faces several challenges..., Despite these challenges, Challenges and Legacy, Future Outlook
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:118:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:119:Formulaic "Challenges" sections that acknowledge a problem then immediately reassure the reader things are fine.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:120:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:121:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:122:> Despite its industrial prosperity, Korattur faces challenges typical of urban areas, including traffic congestion and water scarcity. Despite these challenges, with its strategic location and ongoing initiatives, Korattur continues to thrive as an integral part of Chennai's growth.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:123:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:124:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:125:> Traffic congestion increased after 2015 when three new IT parks opened. The municipal corporation began a stormwater drainage project in 2022 to address recurring floods.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:126:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:127:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:128:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:129:## Language and grammar
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:130:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:131:### 7. AI vocabulary words
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:132:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:133:**High-frequency words:** Additionally, align with, crucial, delve, emphasizing, enduring, enhance, fostering, garner, highlight (verb), interplay, intricate/intricacies, key (adjective), landscape (abstract noun), pivotal, showcase, tapestry (abstract noun), testament, underscore (verb), valuable, vibrant, realm, harness, illuminate, facilitate, bolster, streamline, shed light on, revolutionize, innovative, cutting-edge, game-changing, transformative, seamless/seamlessly, genuinely, actually (as filler intensifier), land/lands/landed (as metaphor for "how something is received"), surface/surfaced (as metaphor for "appears in the discourse"), move/moves/the move (as meta-rhetoric about what writing or argument is doing), unspoken, hidden (when used to inflate significance of something ordinary), unparalleled, invaluable, bolstered, meticulous
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:134:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:135:**AI transition phrases** (a whole category AI overuses): "that being said", "at its core", "to put it simply", "a key takeaway is", "from a broader perspective", "in today's fast-paced world", "as technology continues to evolve", "but here's..."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:136:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:137:These words and phrases appear far more frequently in post-2023 text than in human writing from earlier periods. They often cluster in the same paragraph, which is a strong tell. No single word is proof, but density is: three or more in one paragraph is a fingerprint.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:138:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:139:**Soft scaffold phrases:** "One useful area...", "Another useful area...", "The main strength...", "The main risk...", "Good use usually comes down to..." These phrases are not flashy, which is why they survive rewrites. They mark a generated explainer that is arranging information into bland labelled blocks instead of writing from a real line of thought.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:140:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:141:**Tentative high-ratio phrase list:** GPTZero's April 2026 AI Vocabulary page is based on 3.3 million texts and exposes 100 public table rows. Use all 100 as clustering signals only. A single phrase is not proof of AI writing, but several in one paragraph is a strong smell.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:142:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:143:Full GPTZero phrase list used by the grader:

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:245:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:246:**Kobak excess vocabulary:** The grader also loads the full 900-row `kobak-excess-words.csv` file from Kobak et al.'s `llm-excess-vocab` repository. The file includes `style`, `content`, `content/style`, and `other` annotations. The aggregate pressure check uses style-annotated terms as one vocabulary signal alongside the local AI-vocabulary list and all 100 GPTZero phrases. Kobak words do not fail text by themselves.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:247:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:248:Current threshold: vocabulary pressure contributes points to an overall score. The overall score only trips when vocabulary pressure combines with structural signals such as manufactured insight, contrived reframes, paragraph uniformity, unrequested headings, soft scaffolding, or assistant residue. This follows the paper's corpus-level logic: excess vocabulary is evidence in a pattern, not a standalone detector verdict.

An enduring testament to Italian colonial influence is the widespread adoption of pasta in the local culinary landscape, showcasing how these dishes have integrated into the traditional diet.

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:257:### 8. Copula avoidance
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:258:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:259:**Words to watch:** serves as/stands as/marks/represents [a], boasts/features/offers [a]
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:260:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:261:Substitutes elaborate constructions for simple "is", "are", or "has".
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:262:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:263:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:264:> Gallery 825 serves as LAAA's exhibition space for contemporary art. The gallery features four separate spaces and boasts over 3,000 square feet.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:265:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:266:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:267:> Gallery 825 is LAAA's exhibition space for contemporary art. The gallery has four rooms totalling 3,000 square feet.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:268:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:269:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:270:### 9. Contrived contrast / negative parallelism
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:271:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:272:Constructions like "Not only...but..." or "It's not just about..., it's..." are overused to the point of being a reliable AI fingerprint. Treat this as a rhetorical move, not a string pattern: the problem is the fake reframe where a simple idea is rejected so a grander, more abstract idea can be revealed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:273:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:274:Agents often dodge the rule by flipping the syntax around. These are the same tell:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:275:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:276:- "It's not X, it's Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:277:- "It's Y, not X."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:278:- "Less X than Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:279:- "More Y than X."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:280:- "Not so much X as Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:281:- "Beyond X, it is Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:282:- "You might think X. Actually, Y."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:283:- "No X. No Y. Just Z."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:284:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:285:Do not preserve the structure by swapping words, reversing the order, replacing "not" with "beyond", or splitting the contrast across sentences. If the sentence works by first rejecting a flat interpretation and then unveiling a supposedly deeper one, rewrite it as a direct claim with evidence.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:286:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:287:**Tolerance note:** Not every contrast is a problem. "The laptop is powerful, not cheap" is a concrete distinction. "The problem is not collaboration. The problem is performative attendance" can be a real argument. The tell is the inflated reveal: a plain interpretation is dismissed so an abstract payload ("meaning", "identity", "humanity", "trust", "belonging") can arrive with fake depth.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:288:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:289:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:290:> It's not just about the beat riding under the vocals; it's part of the aggression and atmosphere. It's not merely a song, it's a statement.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:291:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:292:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:293:> The heavy beat adds to the aggressive tone throughout the track.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:294:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:295:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:296:> The film is a negotiation with memory, not just a family story. Beyond grief, it becomes a meditation on what it means to belong.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:297:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:298:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:299:> The film uses family conflict and remembered details to show how belonging changes after someone dies.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:300:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:301:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:302:> You might think the app is about saving time. Actually, it is about trust.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:303:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:304:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:305:> The app earns trust by showing exactly what changed and letting users undo each step.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:306:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:307:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:308:### 10. Rule of three
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:309:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:310:Ideas forced into groups of three to appear comprehensive, even when the items do not naturally form a triad.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:311:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:312:**Tolerance note:** Three-part structures are common in human rhetoric, comedy, fiction, speeches, and criticism. Treat this as an AI tell when triads cluster densely, feel interchangeable, or use abstract nouns to simulate breadth. Preserve when the three items are concrete, necessary, funny, rhythmic, or part of a voiced argument.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:313:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:314:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:315:> The event features keynote sessions, panel discussions, and networking opportunities. Attendees can expect innovation, inspiration, and industry insights.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:316:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:317:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:318:> The event includes talks and panels, with time for informal networking between sessions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:319:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:320:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:321:### 11. Synonym cycling
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:322:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:323:Excessive synonym substitution, cycling through different words for the same referent within a short span due to repetition-penalty mechanisms.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:324:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:325:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:326:> The protagonist faces many challenges. The main character must overcome obstacles. The central figure eventually triumphs. The hero returns home.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:327:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:328:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:329:> The protagonist faces many challenges but eventually triumphs and returns home.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:330:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:331:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:332:### 12. False ranges
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:333:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:334:"From X to Y" constructions where X and Y are not on a meaningful scale, creating an illusion of breadth without communicating scope.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:335:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:336:**Before:**

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:392:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:393:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:394:### 17. Curly quotation marks
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:395:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:396:**Tolerance note:** Curly quotes are typography, not inherently AI writing. Normalise them in hard-mode plain output if requested. Preserve them in sourced excerpts, literary fixtures, publication text, or quoted material.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:397:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:398:ChatGPT uses curly quotes instead of straight quotes.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:399:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:400:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:401:> He said \u201cthe project is on track\u201d but others disagreed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:402:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:403:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:404:> He said "the project is on track" but others disagreed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:405:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:406:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:407:### 18. Hyphenated compound modifier overuse
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:408:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:409:**Words to watch when clustered:** third-party, cross-functional, client-facing, data-driven, decision-making, well-known, high-quality, real-time, long-term, end-to-end
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:410:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:411:Individual hyphenations are often correct, but AI stacks four or five in a single sentence. When you encounter three or more hyphenated compound modifiers in one sentence, restructure to reduce the density.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:412:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:413:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:414:> The cross-functional team delivered a high-quality, data-driven report on our client-facing tools. Their decision-making process was well-known for being thorough and detail-oriented.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:415:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:416:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:417:> The team, drawn from several departments, delivered a report grounded in usage data for our client-facing tools. Their process for making decisions was known for being thorough.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:418:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:419:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:420:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:421:## Communication
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:422:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:423:### 19. Collaborative artifacts
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:424:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:425:**Words to watch:** I hope this helps, Of course!, Certainly!, You're absolutely right!, Would you like..., let me know, here is a...
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:426:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:427:Chatbot correspondence pasted directly into content without being cleaned up.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:428:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:429:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:430:> Here is an overview of the French Revolution. I hope this helps! Let me know if you'd like me to expand on any section.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:431:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:432:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:433:> The French Revolution began in 1789 when financial crisis and food shortages led to widespread unrest.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:434:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:435:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:436:### 20. Knowledge-cutoff disclaimers
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:437:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:438:**Words to watch:** as of [date], Up to my last training update, While specific details are limited/scarce..., based on available information...
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:439:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:440:AI disclaimers about incomplete information left in the text.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:441:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:442:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:443:> While specific details about the company's founding are not extensively documented in readily available sources, it appears to have been established sometime in the 1990s.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:444:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:445:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:446:> The company was founded in 1994, according to its registration documents.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:447:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:448:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:449:### 21. Sycophantic/servile tone
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:450:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:451:Overly positive, people-pleasing language that performs agreement rather than engaging with substance.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:452:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:453:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:454:> Great question! You're absolutely right that this is a complex topic. That's an excellent point about the economic factors.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:455:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:456:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:457:> The economic factors you mentioned are relevant here, particularly the trade deficit data from Q3.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:458:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:459:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:460:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:461:## Filler and hedging
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:462:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:463:### 22. Filler phrases
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:464:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:465:Common substitutions:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:466:- "In order to achieve this goal" -> "To achieve this"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:467:- "Due to the fact that it was raining" -> "Because it was raining"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:468:- "At this point in time" -> "Now"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:469:- "In the event that you need help" -> "If you need help"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:470:- "The system has the ability to process" -> "The system can process"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:471:- "It is important to note that the data shows" -> "The data shows"
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:472:- "In today's fast-paced world" -> cut entirely
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:473:- "As technology continues to evolve" -> cut entirely
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:474:- "At the end of the day" -> cut or replace with specific conclusion
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:475:- "Generally speaking" -> "Usually" or cut
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:476:- "Broadly speaking" -> "Overall" or cut
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:477:- "From a broader perspective" -> cut or state the perspective directly
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:478:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:479:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:480:### 23. Excessive hedging
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:481:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:482:Over-qualifying statements to the point where the sentence commits to nothing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:483:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:484:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:485:> It could potentially possibly be argued that the policy might have some effect on outcomes.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:486:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:487:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:488:> The policy may affect outcomes.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:489:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:490:### 23a. False concession hedges
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:491:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:492:**Words to watch:** "While critics argue..., supporters say...", "the truth lies somewhere in the middle", "both sides have valid points", "it depends on context" when used to avoid a claim.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:493:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:494:AI often performs nuance by staging two generic positions and then landing in a bland middle. Real nuance names the evidence, stakes, and tradeoffs. If the sentence only balances abstractions, rewrite it as a direct claim.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:495:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:496:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:497:> While critics argue remote work weakens culture, supporters say it improves flexibility. The truth lies somewhere in the middle.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:498:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:499:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:500:> Remote work improves flexibility for most desk workers, but it exposes weak management habits that office routines used to hide.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:501:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:502:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:503:### 24. Generic positive conclusions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:504:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:505:Vague upbeat endings that could be appended to any article on any topic.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:506:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:507:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:508:> The future looks bright for the company. Exciting times lie ahead as they continue their journey toward excellence. This represents a major step in the right direction.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:509:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:510:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:511:> The company plans to open two more locations next year, both in the southeast.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:512:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:513:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:514:### 25. Staccato rhythm in extended contexts
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:515:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:516:This supplements the hard constraint on staccato fragments. Beyond the obvious standalone fragments, watch for the subtler pattern: AI places short sentences at the start of sections (to hook), at the end of paragraphs (to land), and in sequences of two or three (to build momentum). These positions are predictable.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:517:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:518:**Tolerance note:** Staccato is not automatically bad. Preserve it when it is character voice, panic, comedy, dialogue, aphorism, or deliberate literary rhythm. Cut it when it functions as generic article emphasis.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:519:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:520:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:521:> The data was clear. Unmistakably so. Every metric pointed in the same direction. And that direction was down.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:522:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:523:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:524:> The data pointed unambiguously in one direction: every metric was declining, and the trend had been consistent for three consecutive quarters.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:525:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:526:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:527:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:528:## Sensory and atmospheric
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:529:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:530:These patterns are especially common in descriptive, creative, and reflective writing. AI reaches for sensory language but, having no physical experience, attaches it to abstractions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:531:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:532:### 26. Ghost/spectral language
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:533:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:534:**Words to watch:** ghost(s), spectral, shadow(s), whisper(s), echo(es), phantom, haunting/haunted, lingering, remnant(s), trace(s) used atmospherically, unspoken, hidden (when inflating the significance of something ordinary)
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:535:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:536:AI defaults to spectral, ghostly, shadowy imagery for anything it wants to make feel deep. Everything becomes a shadow, a memory, a whisper, or an echo. One "ghost" is fine. Four in two paragraphs is a tell.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:537:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:538:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:539:> The pebbles carry the ghosts of the boulders they were, resting in a quiet space between the earth and the sea. Each one is a whisper from a vanished landscape, an echo of forces that shaped the coastline.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:540:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:541:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:542:> The pebbles are fragments of larger rocks, broken down over centuries by water and weather. They collect in the spaces between tide lines where the current drops them.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:543:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:544:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:545:### 27. Quietness obsession
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:546:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:547:**Words to watch:** quiet/quietly, silent/silently, softly, hum/humming, stillness, gentle, hushed, murmur, settle/settled, tender
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:548:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:549:AI inserts quietness and softness where it does not belong, often against the logic of the scene. In a 759-word essay about pebbles, one AI used "quiet" ten times. The word has become a proxy for depth.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:550:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:551:Adjacent manufactured-insight frames include "when no one noticed", "the shift nobody noticed", "before anyone noticed", and "without anyone noticing". These usually imply privileged perception without doing the evidentiary work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:552:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:553:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:554:> There is a quiet beauty in the way the morning light settles on the table. The coffee hums softly in its cup. Outside, the world has a gentle stillness to it, as if holding its breath.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:555:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:556:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:557:> The morning light was on the table. The coffee was getting cold. Outside, a truck reversed into the loading bay and someone dropped a crate.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:558:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:559:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:560:### 28. Forced synesthesia
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:561:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:562:AI blends senses inappropriately to simulate literary depth: emotions get tastes, sounds get colours, abstract concepts get textures. This happens because the model has no physical experience, so its sensory vocabulary gravitates to immaterial subjects. Real synesthetic writing is specific and grounded ("a great plateful of blue water" works because Woolf had both stood before a view and sat down to a meal). AI synesthesia is unanchored.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:563:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:564:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:565:> Thursday is a liminal day that tastes of almost-Friday. Her grief hummed with the colour of old photographs. The silence had a texture, rough and amber, draped across the room like forgotten cloth.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:566:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:567:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:568:> Thursday felt like waiting. She kept pulling out old photographs and putting them back. The room was silent in a way that made her aware of her own breathing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:569:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:570:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:571:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:572:## Structural tells
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:573:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:574:### 29. Mid-sentence rhetorical questions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:575:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:576:Short questions dropped mid-paragraph as a pacing device, followed by a declarative answer. Borrowed from long-form journalism but deployed indiscriminately. Includes the "The real X?" pattern: "The real insight?", "The real challenge?", "The takeaway?", "The kicker?" These perform revelation through question format.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:577:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:578:**Tolerance note:** Interviews, teaching prose, polemic, and comic essays often use questions legitimately. Treat as an AI tell when the answer is immediate, generic, and framed as revelation.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:579:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:580:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:581:> But now? You won't be able to unsee this one. The solution? It's simpler than you think. The real question? Whether we're ready to face it.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:582:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:583:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:584:> Once you notice it, you will keep noticing it. The solution is simpler than most people assume, though whether anyone is ready to act on it is a separate issue.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:585:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:586:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:587:### 30. Generic/ungrounded metaphors
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:588:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:589:AI metaphors are plausible but specific to nobody. They gesture toward meaning without achieving it. Human metaphors draw from personal experience or shared cultural references. AI metaphors draw from the statistical middle.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:590:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:591:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:592:> Learning the ukulele is like teaching your fingers to dance again after years of sitting still. Every chord is a puzzle piece that finally clicks into a song.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:593:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:594:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:595:> The first week my fingers could not stretch far enough for a G chord and I kept muting the string next to it with the side of my index finger.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:596:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:597:When you spot a metaphor, ask: could anyone have written this, or does it come from a specific experience? If anyone could have written it, replace it with a concrete detail.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:598:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:599:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:600:### 31. Excessive list-making
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:601:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:602:AI converts prose to bullet points when the content does not warrant it. This is driven by RLHF training: human raters reward structured-looking answers, so the model learns that bullets = quality. The result is text that looks organised but reads like a slide deck rather than writing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:603:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:604:When you encounter unnecessary bullet points or numbered lists, fold the content back into prose. Lists are appropriate for genuinely discrete items (ingredients, steps, specifications). They are not appropriate for flowing arguments, observations, or narrative.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:605:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:606:### 31a. Unicode flair
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:607:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:608:**Words/symbols to watch:** arrows, checkmarks, stars, ornamental bullets, emoji-style symbols in prose or professional content.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:609:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:610:Decorative Unicode makes prose look like a generated checklist or social post. Remove it unless the symbols are part of a real UI, quoted material, or an actual checklist whose visual form matters.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:611:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:612:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:613:### 32. Dramatic narrative transitions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:614:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:615:**Words to watch:** "Something shifted.", "Everything changed.", "And then, everything clicked.", "That's when it hit me.", "And that made all the difference."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:616:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:617:Standalone sentences that claim a narrative turning point without earning it. These borrow the structure of memoir writing ("The door opened and my life was never the same") but deploy it for mundane observations. They combine staccato fragments with manufactured insight.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:618:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:619:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:620:> I had been thinking about productivity all wrong. And then, something shifted. I stopped optimising for output and started optimising for energy. Everything changed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:621:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:622:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:623:> I stopped trying to do more in less time and started paying attention to when I had energy and when I did not. The change was not dramatic, but over a few months the difference was obvious in my work.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:624:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:625:### 38. Section scaffolding
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:626:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:627:**Words to watch:** Identical subheadings repeated across sections ("How to make this work:", "Key takeaways:", "Why this matters:")
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:628:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:629:AI-generated articles with numbered sections often repeat the same structural label in each section, creating a cookie-cutter template. The repetition itself is the tell — human writers vary their section structure and rarely use identical action labels across multiple sections.

/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:654:---
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:655:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:656:## Voice and register
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:657:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:658:These patterns concern what AI **removes** from writing — stance, personality, specificity — rather than what it adds. Based on Abdulhai et al. (2026), "How LLMs Distort Our Written Language", which showed LLMs systematically strip argumentative commitment (~70% neutrality increase) and personal voice (50% pronoun depletion) even in minimal edits.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:659:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:660:### 33. Countdown negation
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:661:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:662:**Words to watch:** "It wasn't X. It wasn't Y. It was Z.", "This isn't about... This isn't about... This is about..."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:663:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:664:A multi-sentence rhetorical arc where AI negates two or more things before revealing the actual point, creating false suspense. Distinct from negative parallelism (#9), which is "not X; it's Y" in a single sentence. This is a sustained dramatic build.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:665:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:666:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:667:> It wasn't the algorithm. It wasn't the data. It wasn't the compute budget. It was the prompt. Three words, chosen carefully, changed everything.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:668:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:669:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:670:> The improvement came from rewriting the prompt, not from changes to the model or data. The team had spent weeks on architecture changes before trying this.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:671:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:672:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:673:> This isn't about technology. This isn't about efficiency. This is about what it means to be human in an automated world.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:674:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:675:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:676:> The automation question is less about the technology itself and more about how it changes the day-to-day work that people build their identity around.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:677:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:678:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:679:### 34. Per-paragraph miniature conclusions
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:680:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:681:Every paragraph wraps up with a tidy summary sentence that transitions perfectly to the next. Humans digress, leave threads hanging, circle back later. AI's paragraph-level tidiness is itself a tell.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:682:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:683:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:684:> The study surveyed 400 teachers across 12 districts. Most reported increased workload since 2020. The takeaway is clear: teachers are stretched thin and the trend shows no signs of reversing.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:685:>
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:686:> The funding picture compounds this pressure. Per-pupil spending has risen by 4% nominally but fallen in real terms. This financial squeeze makes the workload problem even harder to address.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:687:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:688:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:689:> The study surveyed 400 teachers across 12 districts. Most reported increased workload since 2020, with marking and administrative tasks growing fastest.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:690:>
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:691:> Per-pupil spending has risen by 4% nominally but fallen in real terms. Whether the two trends are connected is debatable, though several principals I spoke to thought so.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:692:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:693:When you spot a paragraph where the final sentence restates the paragraph's point or transitions smoothly to the next topic, consider cutting it or replacing it with something that leaves a thread open.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:694:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:695:Watch for endings such as "That is why...", "The takeaway is...", "The result is...", "In the end,...", "Ultimately,...", and "With that distinction in mind...". One can be legitimate. Three or more usually means the piece is landing every paragraph the same way.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:696:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:697:Also check paragraph size. AI-generated longform often settles into near-identical blocks: ten paragraphs of 65-85 words, each making one balanced point. Human paragraphs usually show uneven pressure; some are short, some wander, some carry a scene or example longer than expected.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:698:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:699:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:700:### 35. Tonal uniformity / register lock
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:701:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:702:AI picks a register — professional-casual, academic-accessible, warm-but-authoritative — and never breaks from it. Human writers drift between registers: they start formal, get colloquial, make a joke that does not quite land, recover. The consistency is the tell, not any particular register.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:703:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:704:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:705:> The architecture of the system reflects careful consideration of user needs. Each component has been designed with modularity in mind, allowing for straightforward maintenance. The team has prioritised clarity in the API surface, ensuring that developers can integrate with minimal friction.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:706:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:707:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:708:> The system is modular, which mostly works well. The API is clean — I got a prototype running in an afternoon, though I hit a wall with the auth flow that took longer to sort out. The docs say "straightforward" but that is doing some heavy lifting.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:709:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:710:This pattern cannot be caught programmatically. During the self-audit, ask: does the whole text sit in one register? If it reads like a single voice speaking at a single speed about everything, introduce at least one register break — a moment of informality, a parenthetical doubt, a shift in sentence rhythm.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:711:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:712:In reviews and criticism, tonal uniformity often appears as bland evaluative balance: "emotional range", "field of sympathy", "moral strength", "earns its weight", "ambitious in an old-fashioned way", "social texture", "slow revelation". Replace this with concrete claims about scenes, sentences, performances, or formal choices.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:713:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:714:### 35a. Orphaned demonstratives
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:715:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:716:**Words to watch:** "This highlights...", "This underscores...", "This demonstrates...", "That speaks to...", "These point to..."
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:717:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:718:The problem is not the word "this"; it is the vague subject. If "this" points to a whole previous paragraph, replace it with the actual noun or claim.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:719:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:720:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:721:> The team missed the deadline and the launch slipped by three weeks. This highlights the importance of communication.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:722:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:723:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:724:> The missed deadline exposed a communication gap between product and engineering.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:725:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:726:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:727:### 36. Faux specificity
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:728:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:729:AI provides examples that feel specific without actually being specific. "The smell of coffee on a Sunday morning" or "the way the light hits your kitchen table" — plausible, relatable, grounded in nobody's actual experience. AI constructs these from genre conventions rather than lived experience.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:730:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:731:Related to experiential vacancy (see Personality and soul in SKILL.md) but names the active mechanism: AI **performs** specificity rather than achieving it.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:732:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:733:**Before:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:734:> There is something about the way a handwritten letter feels in your hands — the weight of the paper, the slight smudge of ink, the care in every stroke. It reminds you that someone took the time to sit down and think of you.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:735:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:736:**After:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:737:> My grandmother wrote to me every month until she died. Her handwriting got worse each year — by the end I could only read about half the words. I kept every letter in a shoebox under my bed.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:738:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:739:When you spot a "specific" detail, ask: could anyone have written this, or does it come from a particular person's experience? If it reads like a stock photo in prose form, replace it with something that could not have been generated from genre conventions.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:740:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:741:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:742:### 37. Neutrality collapse
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:743:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:744:LLMs systematically strip argumentative stance, defaulting to balanced treatment of everything. "There are compelling arguments on both sides" where the original had a clear position. Distinct from generic positive conclusions (#24) — this is about the **removal** of opinion, not the addition of optimism.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:745:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:746:Abdulhai et al. (2026) found a ~70% increase in essays that remained neutral when writers used LLMs extensively, and that LLMs frequently changed the writer's conclusions even when instructed to only fix grammar.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:747:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:748:**Before (human original):**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:749:> Remote work is better for most knowledge workers. The evidence is overwhelming and the objections are mostly about control, not productivity.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:750:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:751:**After AI "editing":**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:752:> Remote work offers several advantages for knowledge workers, though in-office collaboration also has its merits. The evidence suggests benefits in both directions, and the optimal approach likely depends on the specific context and team dynamics.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:753:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:754:**How to fix:**
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:755:> Remote work is better for most knowledge workers. The productivity data from Stanford and Owl Labs both point the same way, and the main counterarguments — spontaneous collaboration, mentorship, culture — have not held up well in studies that actually measured them.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:756:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:757:When rewriting, compare your rewrite's conclusions to the input's conclusions. If the stance shifted toward neutral, you have introduced the same distortion the research documents. Restore the original position.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:758:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:759:### 39. Template and placeholder residue
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:760:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:761:**Words to watch:** `{client_name}`, `[Company Name]`, `[insert date]`, `<source>`, "Hi {name}".
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:762:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:763:Unfilled placeholders are not style issues; they are generated/template residue. Replace them with real values if known, or remove the surrounding sentence if not.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:764:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:765:### 40. Rubric echoing
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:766:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:767:**Words to watch:** "the author creates a tone", "I can tell because", "this quote shows that", "according to the rubric", "meets the criteria".
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:768:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:769:Common in AI-generated student essays. It mirrors assignment language instead of analysing the text. Preserve only if the piece is explicitly about the rubric.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:770:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:771:### 41. Genre-specific manual checks
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:772:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:773:These are not reliable enough for hard regex treatment yet, but they should be part of the self-audit:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:774:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:775:- **Academic/research:** verify citations, DOIs, dates, journals, reference order, and whether cited works actually support the claim. Plausible citation format is not evidence.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:776:- **Poetry:** watch for default quatrains, unrequested rhyme, first-person plural overuse, mood-word accumulation, and formal gestures that do not follow through.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:777:- **Fiction:** watch for flattened dialogue, "as-you-know" exposition, parenthetical stage directions, locked POV with no pressure, over-resolved endings, and scene pacing that never surprises.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/patterns.md:778:- **Email/business:** watch for placeholders, over-warm openings, fake personalisation, and action lists dressed up with symbols.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:1:# Vetted human alternatives
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:2:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:3:Replacements the Suggestions action draws from when a flagged tell is lexical. Also used by Rewrite and Write when picking replacements during the surface pass.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:4:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:5:## Scope
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:6:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:7:This file covers lexical patterns: patterns where a specific word or phrase can be swapped for a plain-English alternative. Structural patterns (paragraph-length uniformity, anaphoric scaffolding, sentence-length variance, tonal uniformity) do not appear here. Suggestions writes contextual rewriting instructions for those.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:8:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:9:A few patterns that look lexical (contrived contrast, false-concession, bland critical templates) get entries below, but their middle column names what the construction usually *means* rather than offering a phrase to swap. Substitution does not work for those; the construction itself is the problem.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:10:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:11:## What "vetted" means
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:12:
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:13:Each alternative has been reviewed for AI flavour. The right replacement is rarely a lower-temperature synonym ("dig into" for "delve"); it is the plain word that names what the writer actually meant. If the AI word usually means one of three or four specific things, the writer's job is to pick which and use that. Where the most natural fix is to cut the word, the entry says so. Not every flagged word has a defensible replacement.
/Users/mae/Documents/human-eyes/human-eyes/references/alternatives.md:14:
